title: service-accounts — driver specifications
description: Two driver contracts. `SecretRotator` wraps password-style bcrypt hashing for the rotation secret (single driver — bcrypt; alternative drivers reserved for future argon2id migration). `ServiceAccountJwtIssuer` bridges to the auth module's JwtSigner — there is no local JWT-driver family; this module NEVER signs JWTs directly.

methods.returns: Cryptographically random plaintext secret (base64-encoded). Default 32 random bytes = 256 bits of entropy.
Bcrypt hash at cost 12. Same algorithm as Laravel Hash::make with rounds=12.
Constant-time comparison. Delegates to Laravel Hash::check. Never leaks per-input timing.
True when the hash cost is below the current `service-accounts.security.hash_cost` config value. Enables gradual cost migration without breaking existing rows.

IssuedToken { token: string, expires_at: DateTimeImmutable, jti: string }. Composes claims: sub = $sa->id, app = $sa->application_id (slug form), tid = $sa->tenant_id (when non-null), aud = $scope->aud (or SA default), iat + exp, jti (fresh UUID). Signs via auth::JwtSigner::forKid($sa->signer_kid). Fires ServiceAccountJwtIssued event.
Decoded + verified claims. Delegates to auth::JwtVerifier for signature + iss + aud + exp, then confirms the SA row is enabled + not expired. Refuses on any inconsistency.
Collection of SAs whose last_used_at is older than `service-accounts.security.dormant_threshold_days`. Scopes to one application when applicationId is provided; otherwise iterates every application.

FingerprintDelta { is_novel_tuple: bool, current_rate: float, baseline_rate: float, rate_spike_ratio: float }. Populates the rolling 30-day fingerprint cache; computes the delta for the audit middleware to decide whether to dispatch SuspiciousServiceAccountActivityNotification.
Whether the tuple has been observed in the rolling 30-day window.

secret_rotator_drivers.name: bcrypt
strengths: Battle-tested; deterministic; ships with every PHP install

Sufficient for machine-credential storage where memory-hardness isn't critical (SA secrets rotate frequently + are randomly generated, so GPU attacks against a stolen hash are less impactful than password-hash breaches)
weaknesses: Not memory-hard (unlike argon2id which we DO use for Identity password hashes)

cost.description: Bcrypt rounds. 12 = ~250ms per hash on modern hardware — acceptable on the exchange-endpoint hot path with a rate limit of 5 requests per minute per SA.

jwt_issuer_drivers.name: hs256-via-auth
jwt_issuer_drivers.underlying_package: Stackra\Auth\Services\JwtSigner (owned by identity/auth) + firebase/php-jwt (owned by auth transitively)
strengths: Reuses the audited signing routine — no local re-implementation of JWT signing
Kid rotation + revocation live entirely in the auth module; this module ships only the FK
When Wave 1c adds RS256 alongside HS256, no changes here — the auth module's JwtSigner picks the algorithm from the kid's `algorithm` column
weaknesses: Introduces a runtime dependency on the identity/auth module. `identity/service-accounts` module priority (24) must sit above `identity/auth` (25) so the SA row exists before auth wires up the signer — but auth calls into this module only when a request presents a SA JWT, which is post-boot.
